title: Vegetable Pakoda

ingredients.title: For the batter
items: 1 cup chickpea flour (besan) — sifted for smooth texture
1 tsp ajwain seeds (carom seeds) — helps digestion, adds aroma
½–1 tsp red chili powder (to taste)

ingredients.title: Vegetables
items: ½ cup carrot — julienned or grated
½ cup cauliflower florets — small bite-sized pieces
1 tbsp fresh spinach — optional, chopped for color variation

ingredients.title: For frying
items: Neutral oil (mustard oil for traditional flavor or sunflower/peanut oil) — enough for deep frying

instruction.title: Method
steps: Prep the vegetables: Slice onion thin, shred cabbage, julienne carrots, and break cauliflower into small florets. Pat dry if watery — helps crispness.
Make the batter: In a large mixing bowl, combine chickpea flour, rice flour, ajwain seeds, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, and salt.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, and coriander leaves. Mix dry.
Combine vegetables with batter: Add all vegetables to the dry mix and toss to coat evenly before adding water.
Add water little by little until you get a thick clinging batter — not runny.
Fry pakodas in small batches, dropping irregular scoops into hot oil.
Fry until golden brown (3–4 minutes), turning occasionally.
Drain on paper towels and sprinkle chaat masala if desired.
Serve hot with green chutney or tamarind chutney.

chefTips: Keep batter thick — this ensures crisp, craggy pakodas.
Do not overcrowd the pan while frying.
Irregular shapes create better crunch and texture.
